title: Insecure temporary file creation methods should be avoided
description: Creating temporary files in a non-atomic way introduces race condition issues in the application’s behavior. Indeed, a third party can create a given file between when the application chooses its name and when it creates it. In such a situation, the application might use a temporary file that it does not entirely control.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects insecure temporary file creation patterns using predictable names or paths
logic.triggering_conditions: (Path.GetTempPath() with manual file name construction) OR (Hardcoded temp paths) OR (Predictable file names with DateTime)
logic.reasoning: This rule detects insecure temporary file creation that can lead to race conditions. Pattern matches: (1) Path.GetTempPath() + hardcoded names, (2) hardcoded temp paths like C:\temp\, (3) predictable DateTime-based names. Excludes secure methods: GetTempFileName() and GetRandomFileName(). AST-based approach examines Assignment nodes for insecure temp file patterns.
